text:
Once upon a time, in a small coastal town, there was a peculiar bookshop that stood like a ship at the edge of the tide. The owner, an eccentric old man named Mr. Jenkins, would rummage through forgotten corners of his library for stories that seemed to sing when he read them aloud.

The shop was known for its peculiar collection. Mr. Jenkins would sometimes tell visitors that the tales were "spirited"—the ink on pages moved, the words spoke, and the walls came alive. One especially enchanting afternoon, while browsing the shelves, an old, green tome caught his eye. Its title read "The Chronicles of Whispers."

After admiring the dust jacket, Mr. Jenkins carefully opened the book. The pages shimmered as if bathed in moonlight. On the first page, there was a map of the old seaside town.

"The story begins at the lighthouse," it read. "Follow the light and you'll find the answers you seek."
